title: The Last Train
introKo:
야근 끝에 아슬아슬하게 잡은 막차.
옆자리 노인이 조용히 말을 걸어와요.

quiz.q: 옆자리 노인은 이 막차를 얼마나 오래 탔다고 했나요?

easy.text: It is late at night. Work is over. You run to the station.
You catch the last train. The doors close behind you. The car is almost empty.

options.reaction: You close your eyes. The train moves softly. Your body feels heavy.
The city lights go by. The night is quiet.

easy.text: An old man sits next to you. "Long day?" he asks.
He smiles. "I took this train every night for forty years," he says. "I still take it sometimes. You look very tired."

options.reaction: He nods slowly. "I know that feeling well," he says.
"Your eyes," he laughs. "My eyes were like that, too."

easy.text: You talk about work and life. His words are warm and slow.
"When I was young, I only worked," he says. "Now I remember the small things, not the long nights."
You think about his words. Then the train stops. It is your station. The old man smiles at you.

options.reaction: "You too," he says. "And eat a warm dinner."
"Same train, same time," he laughs. The doors open.

easy.text: You walk home under the night sky. The city is quiet. His words stay with you, like a small light in the dark.

normal.text: It is almost midnight when you finish work. You run through the empty streets to the station.
You catch the last train just before the doors close. The car is almost empty, and your heart beats fast.

options.reaction: You close your eyes and let the day go. The train moves softly under you, and slowly your tired body begins to feel heavy and warm.
The city lights pass by outside the window, one after another. From the warm train, the night looks quiet and far away.

normal.text: An old man sits down next to you. "Long day?" he asks with a small smile.
"I took this train every night for forty years," he says. "Even now, I still ride it from time to time. And I know that tired look."

options.reaction: He nods slowly. "I had many days like that," he says. "More than I can count."
"Your eyes," he laughs. "A long time ago, my eyes looked just like that after work."

normal.text: You talk about work and life. He listens carefully, and his slow, warm words make the late train feel less cold.
"When I was young, I only thought about work," he says. "But when I look back now, I remember the small warm things, not the long nights at the office."
You are still thinking about his words when the train slows down and stops at your station. The old man looks up at you and smiles.

options.reaction: "You too," he says. "And don't go to bed hungry — eat something warm tonight."
"Same train, same time," he laughs. "That is how old friends are made." The doors open behind you.

normal.text: You walk home under the quiet night sky. The city sleeps, but his words stay with you, small and warm, like a light in the dark.

hard.text: It is nearly midnight by the time you finish work, and you run through the sleeping streets toward the station.
You catch the last train just as the doors slide shut behind you. The car is nearly empty, and your heart is still beating fast.

options.reaction: You sink into the seat and close your eyes. The train moves gently under you, and your tired body finally feels heavy.
The city lights slip past outside the window, far away and quiet, like another world.

hard.text: An old man settles into the seat next to you. "Long day?" he asks, with a smile that seems to know the answer.
"I took this train every night for forty years," he says. "Even now that my working days are over, I still find myself riding it now and then. And I know that tired look better than anyone."

options.reaction: He nods slowly, as if he can see the whole day on your face. "I know that feeling very well," he says.
"Your eyes," he laughs quietly. "Mine looked exactly like that once, a long time ago."

hard.text: The two of you talk about work and life while the dark city rolls past the windows. He listens carefully, and his slow, warm words make the late train feel less empty.
"When I was young, I did nothing but work," he says. "These days I remember the small warm moments, not the long nights I spent at the office."
You are still turning his words over in your mind when the train slows and stops at your station. The old man looks up at you with a gentle smile.

options.reaction: "You too," he says warmly. "And make sure you eat a proper warm dinner tonight."
"Same train, same time," he laughs as the doors open in front of you.

hard.text: You walk home under the quiet night sky, through streets that are finally still. The city sleeps around you, but his words stay close, small and warm, like a single light left on in the dark.

anyone.ko: 누구, 그 누구 (better than anyone: 누구보다 잘)

count.ko: 세다, 헤아리다 (more than I can count: 셀 수 없이 많이)

left.ko: 켜 둔 (leave의 과거분사, left on: 켜 놓은)

let.ko: ~하게 두다 (let the day go: 하루를 흘려보내다)

safe.ko: 무사히, 안전하게 (get home safe: 조심히 들어가다)

took.ko: 탔다 (take의 과거, take a train: 기차를 타다)

turning.ko: 곱씹고 있는 (turn over in one's mind: 마음속으로 곱씹다)

introEn:
You barely catch the last train after overtime.
The old man in the next seat starts to talk.
